mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase discount rates for equity valuations, mechanically compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hike sequences with p < 0.05, or rolling Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >65% of triggered months, or event study alpha is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with Rank IC > 0.04, or interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side contraction that outweighs demand resilience, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day forward returns in >60% of cases, or regime-conditional alpha is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation prints exceeding 0.3% trigger immediate expectations of aggressive central bank intervention, causing sharp negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are non-negative in >70% of high-inflation months, or event window returns show no statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with stable or declining unemployment rates confirm robust labor market expansion, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth alongside falling UNRATE fails to generate positive 20-day returns with Rank IC > 0.03, or interaction effect is statistically null.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly translates to higher corporate earnings expectations, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth quarters show negative or flat 60-day forward returns in >55% of cases, or rolling predictive R-squared remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contractions exceeding 0.3% monthly signal immediate supply chain bottlenecks and output gaps, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of contraction months, or event study alpha is statistically indistinguishable from zero.
